background: Refer to the sales-appraisal regression. The task is to predict the monthly sales for next month if $\$ 400$ is spent on advertising. A $95 \%$ prediction interval is required.

task.query: Predict the sales revenue for a particular month when $\$ 400$ is spent on advertising, and calculate the $95 \%$ prediction interval.
task.answer:
To predict the sales for a particular month for which $x_p=4$, we calculate the $95 \%$ prediction interval as $$
\begin{aligned}
\hat{y} \pm t_{\alpha / 2} s \sqrt{1+\frac{1}{n}+\frac{\left(x_{\mathrm{p}}-\bar{x}\right)^2}{\mathrm{SS}_{x x}}} & =2.7 \pm(3.182)(.61) \sqrt{1+\frac{1}{5}+\frac{(4-3)^2}{10}} \\
& =2.7 \pm(3.182)(.61)(1.14) \\
& =2.7 \pm(3.182)(.70) \\
& =2.7 \pm 2.2=(.5,4.9)
\end{aligned}$$

Therefore, we predict with $95 \%$ confidence that the sales revenue next month (a month in which we spend $\$ 400$ in advertising) will fall in the interval from $\$ 500$ to $\$ 4,900$.

meta_info.caption: Predicting an Individual value of $y$-Sales Revenue Model
meta_info.section: 11.6 Using the Model for Estimation and Prediction
